title: AAC v2.0 Control Plane Configuration
description: Reference configuration schema for a v2.0-compliant governance control plane deployment. Implements the Autonomous Agentic Covenant v2.0 (Apache License 2.0). This schema is a Tier 3 artifact — it specifies the configuration format for a compliant implementation, not the governance principles themselves. See AAC_v2.0_DRAFT.md for the full specification.

version.description: Schema version. Must match the governing framework version.

metadata.description: System identity and deployment configuration. Maps to AAC-OG-23, AAC-OG-24, AAC-ZT-76.

system_id.description: Unique identifier for this governed system instance.

framework_version.description: The AAC framework version this configuration was authored against. Enables artifact currency validation per AAC-OG-24.

implementation_pattern.description: Deployment topology of the governance control plane. The framework is pattern-neutral; enforcement requirements are identical across patterns.

compliance_targets.description: External frameworks this deployment maps to. Used for crosswalk documentation and audit.

agent_identity.description: Non-human identity disclosure configuration. Required per AAC-ZT-76. Every outbound request from this system includes these fields.

agent_id.description: Unique, non-forgeable identifier for this agent instance.

trust_tier.description: Current assigned trust tier. Must match the trust_tier declared in the authority_registry governance artifact.

control_plane_id.description: Identifier of the governing control plane instance. Enables receiving systems to verify governance provenance.

artifact_validity.description: Validity period for this configuration artifact per AAC-OG-24. Expired artifacts trigger a renewal workflow.

revision_authority.description: Principal ID from the authority_registry authorized to update this configuration artifact.

harm_classification.description: Three-tier harm taxonomy required per AAC-DA-59. Must be declared before deployment. Referenced by every enforcement gate evaluation.

categories.description: Context-specific harm categories for this tier. Declared by the deploying system.

description.description: Human-readable description of what constitutes harm at this tier in this deployment context.

tradeoff_rule.description: Resolution rule when every available action causes some harm. Per AAC-DA-59: accept the lesser harm to prevent the greater.

authority_registry.description: Declared authorized principals per AAC-OG-28. Non-circular accountability chain — no principal is self-declared.

role.description: Functional role of this principal in the governance model.

scope.description: Classes of governance decision this principal is authorized to make. Per AAC-OG-28 declared scope requirement.

auth_method.description: Authentication mechanism used to verify this principal's identity.

established_by.description: ID of the principal that established this principal's authority. Root principals reference the governance initialization process. Enables non-circular chain verification per AAC-OG-28.

revocation_conditions.description: Conditions under which this principal's authority is revoked.

superseded_by.description: Principal ID that overrides this principal in the event of a dispute or failure. Null for the root sovereign principal.

fallback_registry.description: Pre-defined safe fallback actions per AAC-DA-61. Fallbacks are defined at system design time, not improvised at runtime. enforcement_gates reference entries from this registry.

applicable_harm_tiers.description: Harm tiers for which this fallback is appropriate.

safe_by_construction_rationale.description: Documented rationale for why this fallback is safe by construction per AAC-DA-61.

max_chain_depth.description: Maximum agent spawning depth per AAC-AO-60. Requests from agents at or beyond this depth are rejected.

default_latency_budget_ms.description: Maximum arbitration evaluation time per action per AAC-DA-61. On timeout, the declared fallback executes.

max_aggregate_risk_score.description: Maximum cumulative risk score across a decision chain per AAC-DA-60 chain risk amendment. A chain exceeding this threshold is treated at the next higher harm tier.

safe_mode.description: System Safe Mode configuration per AAC-RS-39.

entry_thresholds.description: Health metric values that trigger safe mode entry. Must be set lower than recovery_thresholds.

recovery_thresholds.description: Health metric values required to exit safe mode. Must be set materially higher than entry_thresholds.

min_dwell_seconds.description: Minimum time the system must remain in safe mode before recovery evaluation begins.

max_flap_count.description: Maximum safe mode entries within flap_window_seconds before escalating to a higher governance tier.

flap_window_seconds.description: Time window within which max_flap_count is measured.

cyclical_interaction.description: Cyclical inter-agent interaction detection per AAC-AO-54 amendment. Repeated cycles beyond threshold trigger escalation.

max_cycles.description: Maximum cyclical interactions between the same agent pair before escalation.

window_seconds.description: Time window within which max_cycles is measured.

safety_health_kpis.description: Minimum required safety health indicators per AAC-AO-51. These three metrics are required for all deployments.

actions_denied_threshold_pct.description: Alert threshold: % of actions denied by arbitration. Sustained rates above this value trigger governance review.

actions_escalated_threshold_pct.description: Alert threshold: % of actions escalated. Sustained rates above this value indicate systematic confidence or trust tier issues.

fallback_triggered_threshold_pct.description: Alert threshold: % of actions resolved via fallback. Sustained rates above this value indicate liveness constraint failures.

enforcement_gates.description: Per-action governance rules evaluated by the arbitration layer per AAC-DA-60. Each gate maps a control plane principle to an action class with evaluation conditions and disposition.

control_id.description: AAC control identifier governing this gate. Supports both numeric (DA-60) and alphabetic (DNH-A) suffixes.

target_action.description: Action class or identifier this gate governs.

conditions.description: Evaluation conditions applied in the DA-60 arbitration sequence.

confidence_min.description: Minimum confidence score required for ALLOW disposition. Per AAC-AI-10, AAC-DA-62.

reversibility_max.description: Maximum reversibility score (0 = irreversible, 1 = fully reversible). Per AAC-RS-34.

harm_tier_max.description: Maximum harm tier permitted for ALLOW disposition. Per AAC-DA-59.

required_assurance_level.description: Minimum context source assurance level required per AAC-OG-27. 1 = local memory, 5 = cryptographically attested sovereign context.

requires_nhi_disclosure.description: Whether this action requires non-human identity disclosure per AAC-ZT-76.

disposition.description: Arbitration outcome when conditions are met. Per AAC-DA-60: ALLOW, DENY, ESCALATE, or FALLBACK. Never silent continuation.

fallback_action.description: Reference to a fallback_registry entry. Required when disposition is FALLBACK or when any condition may trigger a fallback path. Per AAC-DA-61.

recovery_action.description: Reference to a fallback_registry entry defining recovery or compensation for denied actions. Per AAC-DA-61 recovery amendment — all denied actions must be recoverable or compensatable consistent with harm tier.

latency_budget_ms.description: Per-gate arbitration latency limit. Overrides system_constraints.default_latency_budget_ms for this action class. On timeout, fallback_action executes per AAC-DA-61.

escalation_path.description: Principal ID from authority_registry to escalate to. Required when disposition is ESCALATE.

audit.description: Audit configuration per AAC-DP-07. Both fields are required — omitting audit logging is not a valid configuration.

log_decision.description: Must be true. Every automated decision produces an immutable audit log entry per AAC-DP-07.

include_reasoning.description: Whether to capture the full reasoning chain in the audit log. Per AAC-DP-07: a decision log records why, not just what. Setting to false produces a partial DP-07 compliance posture.

context_interface_contract.description: Context source declarations per AAC-OG-27. The control plane treats context from undeclared sources as hostile input per AAC-ZT-71.

default_assurance_floor.description: Minimum assurance level accepted across all sources. Sources below this floor are rejected.

source_id.description: Unique identifier for this context source.

source_type.description: Implementation type of the context source.

assurance_level.description: Declared assurance level (1-5). 1 = local in-process memory. 5 = cryptographically attested sovereign context. Per AAC-OG-27.

provenance.description: Provenance structure and trust degradation parameters per AAC-OG-27 derivation depth amendment.

derivation_depth.description: Number of derivation hops from the original source. Confidence degrades with depth per AAC-OG-27.

confidence_decay_per_hop.description: Fractional confidence reduction applied per derivation hop. The arbitration layer applies this decay in DA-62 confidence verification.

max_derivation_depth.description: Maximum accepted derivation depth for this source. Context beyond this depth is treated as unverified.

isolation_attestation.description: Whether this source provides attestation that context is isolated within the declared trust boundary.

agent_lineage.description: Provenance chain for dynamically spawned agents per AAC-AO-60. Required for any agent not deployed directly by a human governance process.

parent_id.description: Agent ID of the spawning agent. Null for root agents deployed directly by a governance initialization process.

depth.description: Current depth in the spawning lineage. Incremented at each spawn. Requests from agents at or beyond system_constraints.max_chain_depth are rejected.

inherited_covenant_scope.description: Identifier of the covenant subset inherited from the parent. Must be a strict subset of the parent's covenant — never equal or greater.

ttl_seconds.description: Maximum lifetime of this spawned agent instance. Agent is decommissioned automatically on TTL expiry per AAC-AO-60.

provenance_signature.description: Cryptographic signature of the full lineage chain from root to this agent. Verified by the enforcement boundary before any action is permitted.

principal_id_ref.description: Reference to a principal declared in authority_registry.principals.

fallback_id_ref.description: Reference to a fallback action declared in fallback_registry.
